$comment: Documented-exception ledger for the parity closed loop. SOURCE OF TRUTH = the shots + specs generated from the design; any visual divergence is a BUG to fix by default. This holds ONLY narrow, documented exceptions, each governed by a doc. `exceptions` (FE-side): the FE intentionally does NOT match the mock — match by screen == entry.screen AND (entry.node == '*' OR node startsWith entry.node) AND (entry.kind == '*' OR entry.kind == finding kind); each needs a `source` citing a doc/owner ruling. `coverageExempt` (kit-side): kit nodes intentionally exempt from data-mx-node coverage. Keep MINIMAL — when in doubt, fix the FE to match the design.

exceptions.reason: The kit models each screen as its own MxScaffold (dashboard/screen); the FE factors the scaffold into the shared, persistent AppShell, so it cannot carry a per-screen ValueKey without rebuilding the whole shell on every tab switch. Chrome it provides (appbar/notifications/quick-review) IS keyed there when the Today tab is active.

The kit puts an app-bar overflow menu (import/export/settings) on the library screen. The FE consolidates those actions into the shared language Drawer (opened from the avatar), so there is no duplicate overflow control; adding one would create two paths to the same actions.

The kit shows an inline expanding search dock in the app bar. The FE routes the search button to a dedicated full-screen /search experience (richer filtering than an inline dock), so there is no in-place search-dock node.

The kit's empty library offers a secondary 'Add words' CTA. The FE data model requires a deck before any word can be added, so the empty state offers only 'Create deck' (empty-deck); an 'Add words' action is impossible with zero decks.
